steps.title: Distribute the negatives
steps.content:
Subtracting each parenthesized expression changes the signs of every term inside it.
$$(8x^4+7x^3)-(6x^3+4x^2)-(2x^2+x)$$
$$=8x^4+7x^3-6x^3-4x^2-2x^2-x$$

steps.title: Combine matching powers
steps.content:
Combine the $x^3$ terms and the $x^2$ terms.
$$8x^4+(7x^3-6x^3)+(-4x^2-2x^2)-x$$
$$=8x^4+x^3-6x^2-x$$

steps.title: Match the coefficient
steps.content: Compare this with the form $8x^4+x^3+ax^2-x$. The coefficient of $x^2$ is the value of $a$. $$a=-6$$ The answer is A.
